body.conclusion: For these reasons, Ace Alliance finds the proposed "Blockfrost: Maintenance and Next Generation Indexing" Treasury Withdrawals Governance Action constitutional under Article II.6.1 and Article II.7.1 through II.7.6.
body.counterargumentDiscussion:
A reasonable counterargument arises from Article II.7.1 and concerns the operational-subsidy component of this withdrawal. Approximately $900,000 of the ask is, by the proposer's own description, a subsidy to maintain free-tier infrastructure that Blockfrost has historically funded entirely on its own and that today serves approximately 90% of all free-tier API traffic across the ecosystem. On the strongest reading, asking the Treasury to assume an ongoing operating cost of a private commercial service raises a constitutional question: where the funded purpose is a continuing subsidy rather than a discrete deliverable, the per-period costs-and-expenses specification of Article II.7.1 is harder to evaluate against constitutional terms, and the line between funding ecosystem-critical infrastructure and underwriting a private company's commercial decisions becomes thinner. We acknowledge that this argument has force.

We reject that view on these facts. Article II.7.1 requires specification of the purpose, period, costs, and refund terms of the withdrawal, and the proposal supplies each: the purpose is the maintenance of free-tier API infrastructure for a defined nine-month period, the costs are itemized and tied to quarterly uptime SLA compliance milestones, and unspent or non-disbursed funds return to the Treasury under the standard refund mechanism. Whether the community is comfortable funding the ongoing operational cost of a private operator's free-tier offering is a question for DReps to weigh on the merits, not a constitutional defect; Article II.7 does not prohibit operating-subsidy TWGAs nor require that funded work produce discrete one-time deliverables. The Administrator architecture under Article II.7.5 retains the milestone-based pause-and-return remedy if uptime SLA compliance is not demonstrated in any given quarter.
body.rationaleStatement:
A [PDF version][pdf-link] of this rationale is also made available.

[pdf-link]: ipfs://QmUQLfEVfF5U2yRK7CbNynKvmerrhwcKyYAAoyDqxmjR3U

"Blockfrost: Maintenance and Next Generation Indexing" (gov_action1w0shrfxqwv95kk0v4cn34wylz25a2cmqkq5jpc0e2yrahhqava3qwt8k9fx) is a Treasury Withdrawals Governance Action and is therefore governed by both the general proposal standards in Article II, Section 6 and the additional Treasury Withdrawal standards in Article II, Section 7 of the Cardano Constitution.

This TWGA seeks to withdraw ₳7,916,666 from the Cardano Treasury (approximately $1,900,000 at $0.24 per ada) to fund two complementary components over Q2 through Q4 2026: Project Cayley ($1,000,000), a decentralized slice-indexing architecture that allows SPOs and node operators to index and serve chosen portions of the Cardano chain rather than the full dataset, with a modular multi-chain extension to Bitcoin; and an operational subsidy ($900,000) to maintain the free-tier API infrastructure that Blockfrost has funded since inception and that today serves approximately 90% of all free-tier API traffic. Delivery is structured across six quarterly milestones combining indexer releases, GraphQL provider-API exposure, Bitcoin indexer support, and quarter-by-quarter 99.9% uptime SLA compliance demonstrations for the community free tier. Funds are directed into Intersect's 2025 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract (stake17xzc8pt7fgf0lc0x7eq6z7z6puhsxmzktna7dluahrj6g6ghh5qjr), with subsequent migration to a 2026 TRSC once established, and the withdrawal is administered by Intersect on behalf of Cardano Development Holdings.

The procedural standards of Article II.6 are satisfied: the proposal anchors to an IPFS-hosted document with the on-chain blake2b-256 hash, a content-addressed form "immutable and incapable of being altered after submission," and the metadata provides the title, abstract, justification, and supporting materials required by II.6.2.

The substantive Article II.7 requirements are met as well. Under Article II.7.1, "Treasury Withdrawals actions must specify the terms of the withdrawal," and the proposal specifies the purpose (Project Cayley plus a free-tier operational subsidy), period (Q2 through Q4 2026, with quarterly milestones), itemized costs (₳7,916,666 across Product/Engineering/R&D 42.1%, Security Audits 10.5%, and Ops/Infrastructure 47.4%), and refund circumstances (end-of-period sweep and proportional return on cancelled or reduced deliverables). The disclosure requirement of Article II.7.2 is met by a Prior Treasury Receipts table identifying ₳130,708,860 allocated and ₳78,459,777 withdrawn across IO workstreams, including Blockfrost specifically at ₳1,137,500 (88% of its prior allocation), with each prior allocation tied to its on-chain governance action hash. The 350M Net Change Limit covering Epoch 613 to Epoch 713 is set, and this ₳7,916,666 ask does not exceed it, satisfying Article II.7.3 and Appendix I guardrail TREASURY-02a.

A 3rd Party Assurer funded as part of the withdrawal supports milestone acceptance against the deliverables detailed in the Legal Contract between Input Output and Cardano Development Holdings, satisfying the periodic independent audit and oversight allocation under Article II.7.4. Article II.7.5 is satisfied by Intersect's designation as Administrator, acting on behalf of Cardano Development Holdings, operating through the TRSC and PSSC framework with milestone-based disbursement controls and a five-member external Oversight Committee (Sundae Labs, Cardano Foundation, Dquadrant, Xerberus, NMKR). The custody requirement of Article II.7.6 is satisfied by the proposal's commitment that TRSC and PSSC instances cannot be staked with an SPO and will be delegated to the auto-abstain predefined DRep, with a community-audit dashboard, on the same Intersect TRSC pattern we have accepted as constitutional in prior actions in this family.
body.summary: Ace Alliance finds the proposed "Blockfrost: Maintenance and Next Generation Indexing" Treasury Withdrawals Governance Action constitutional. Rationales are archived at https://github.com/ace-alliance/ace-voting/
